description:
A QA engineer is preparing an automated test environment that already contains the
    directory “/home/user/qa_env/bin”.  
    Your assignment is to create a single, easy-to-read symbolic link so that helper
    scripts can always reach the current tool-set at a predictable location.

    1. Create a symbolic link named “/home/user/tools” that points to
       “/home/user/qa_env/bin”.

    2. Immediately after the link is created, write a log file so that the CI system
       can confirm the link’s target.  
       • Path of the log file: “/home/user/qa_symlink_setup.log”  
       • The log file must contain exactly one line (terminated by a single
         newline character).  
       • That line must be the fully resolved, absolute path to which the link
         points:  

         /home/user/qa_env/bin

       • There must be nothing else in the file: no leading/trailing whitespace,
         no blank lines, no comments.

    The automated grader will check two things:

    a. That “/home/user/tools” exists and is a symbolic link whose target is
       “/home/user/qa_env/bin”.

    b. That “/home/user/qa_symlink_setup.log” exists and its entire content is
       the single line shown above, followed by exactly one UNIX newline (LF).

    You only need normal user privileges; no sudo access is required.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing structure supplied to the container -->
    Directory that already exists before the agent starts:
        /home/user/qa_env/bin      (owned by user, no special files inside)

    Required result after the agent finishes:

    1. Symlink:
        Path      : /home/user/tools
        Type      : symbolic link
        Target    : /home/user/qa_env/bin  (must be absolute, not relative)

    2. Log file:
        Path      : /home/user/qa_symlink_setup.log
        Permissions: 0644 (normal default OK)
        Content   : Exactly the 25-character string
                     “/home/user/qa_env/bin” 
                     followed by a single newline (ASCII 0x0A)
